title: [IMPL] Vendor agrun.js dist bundle into project
verification: preview_start + preview_eval + screenshot: window.Agrun==='object'; createRuntime/defineAction/createIndexedDBSessionStore all 'function'; rootMounted=true; stillLoading=false; 7 nav buttons; no console errors; Batches screen renders fully
notes: User approved vendoring (option 1). Bundle lives at repo ROOT (agrun.js/agrun.min.js, NOT dist/). Vendored agrun.min.js (1.6MB UMD -> window.Agrun) locally to assets/agrun.min.js (not hotlinked). index.html: <script src='assets/agrun.min.js'> added after pdf.js, before </head>. main.jsx boot(): added '&& window.Agrun' to the readiness gate. CRITICAL verified: 1.6MB bundle in the boot gate did NOT brick the app (no infinite 'Opening local workspace'). Confirmed real API names match task assumptions: createRuntime, defineAction, createIndexedDBSessionStore all present. NOTE: bundle is uncommitted (git baseline still untracked + concurrent writer active); commit deferred pending baseline curation.

title: [IMPL] Wrap OpenAI and Gemini adapters as agrun defineAction specs
verification: app boots with new app/agent.jsx loaded; window.DocAgent exposed (object, runDocumentAgent fn); no console errors. Real defineAction API confirmed against live bundle (arity 1) + action-contract.md ({name,description,tier,execute->{control,output,summary}}).
notes: Created app/agent.jsx. Wrapped extraction as a single agrun customAction 'extract_document' (tier 0) built via Agrun.defineAction inside runDocumentAgent. The action closes over the current document, so the planner only decides to call it; execute() runs LLMProviders.ocrExtractWithFallback (preserving strict schema + bbox) and returns {control:'complete', output:doc, summary}. Registered with the runtime via createRuntime({customActions:[...]}). Both openaiBrowserSkill and geminiBrowserSkill confirmed present in the bundle.

title: [IMPL] Replace commitBatch phase-2 one-shot call with runtime.run() agent loop
verification: MOCK PATH VERIFIED: db.jsx commitBatch phase-2 now calls window.DocAgent.runDocumentAgent. 5/5 mock runs returned valid docs via agent_path='mock-direct' (bypassing agrun, since the planner needs an LLM); each had document_id + 12 fields; varied statuses ready/need_review/failed (mock's intentional 8% fail). App boots, no console errors, Batches screen renders fully (screenshot).
live_path: UNVERIFIED — the agrun runtime.run() planner path needs a real provider key + the user's browser to exercise. SAFE-BY-CONSTRUCTION: on ANY error it falls back to direct LLMProviders.ocrExtractWithFallback, preserving strict-schema + bbox extraction. Landed labeled per user option-2.
notes: Real path: createRuntime({skills:[provider browser skill], customActions:[extract_document]}) + runtime.run({provider,apiKey,model,prompt,onStep}); maps result.output->doc, tags agent_path='agrun-loop'. ARCHITECTURAL CAVEAT (documented in app/agent.jsx header): agrun's planner is text-prompt-driven while OCR is image->JSON+bbox, so for SINGLE-SHOT extraction the planner is overhead that just routes to our action — genuine agent value (repair/memory/approval) is T008-T012. reprocessDoc (db.jsx ~line 245) still uses the direct path; could be routed later.

title: [IMPL] Add approval gate for low-confidence documents
reason: Depends on T007 (agent loop not in place). Verification needs a real low-confidence extraction, which needs a real key + user browser.

title: [IMPL] Wire batch-scoped IndexedDB session store for cross-doc memory
reason: ARCHITECTURAL CONFLICT found while building: cross-doc memory needs ONE shared agrun session per batch, but (a) the extract_document action closes over a SINGLE document (per-doc), and (b) commitBatch processes docs in parallel via Promise.all. Sharing one session across concurrent per-doc runs collides with agrun's session-record CAS (ADR-0006). Correct design needs SEQUENTIAL per-batch processing with a shared session + a context-passed doc handle (not a closure) — a real restructure of commitBatch. Building it blind (no key to verify memory flow) risks shipping a wrong concurrency model.
next: Verify T007 live loop with a real key FIRST to observe real agent behavior, then restructure commitBatch to sequential-per-batch with a shared createIndexedDBSessionStore({dbName:'docubridge-batch-'+batchId}) and a context-based doc handle.

title: [IMPL] Add repair loop for low-confidence extracted fields
reason: Depends on T007. Crop-and-re-query repair needs a real provider call to verify.

VERIFIED in preview: tab renders, empty-state 'No agent steps recorded for this document.' shows for seed docs, footnote present, no console errors, screenshot captured.
live_note: Real step streaming needs a live agrun run (unverified, needs key) — but the panel render + empty/populated states are verified. Mock/direct docs show a single explanatory row.

title: [RESEARCH] Evaluate PaddleOCR-VL as a local extraction backend (PRD Phase 2)
verification: websearch (self-hosted SearXNG) on PaddleOCR-VL browser/WASM feasibility + accuracy
notes: FINDINGS: PaddleOCR-VL-1.6 (0.9B VLM, ERNIE-4.5-0.3B + NaViT encoder) is current SOTA document parsing — 96.3% OmniDocBench v1.6, beats GPT-4o on parsing, leads text/formula/table. Official PaddleOCR.js browser SDK runs PP-OCRv5 (the OCR text pipeline) directly in-browser. CAVEAT: the full VL document-parsing model (~0.9B params) is resource-heavy and typically deployed server-side / vLLM, NOT realistic for in-browser WASM inference at acceptable latency. CONCLUSION for this local-first app: PaddleOCR.js (PP-OCRv5 text OCR) is the realistic browser option and could become a third agrun custom action for raw text extraction alongside openai/gemini; the full VL parsing would require a backend, which conflicts with the local-first/no-server constraint. Recommend: keep cloud LLM (Gemini) as the structured+grounded extractor for now; consider PaddleOCR.js only as an offline text-OCR fallback if/when a backend is added. Sources: github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR, paddleocr.ai docs, docs.vllm.ai PaddleOCR-VL recipe.

title: [BUG-P0] refreshBatch sets status='approved' when all docs are 'failed'
notes: db.jsx:116-130 refreshBatch starts with status='approved' and only checks processing/need_review/ready/approved-submitted. When ALL docs are 'failed', none of the checks match and batch shows 'Approved'. Fix: add else-if for has('failed') and all-failed case → set status='failed'.

title: [BUG-P0] Failed docs show placeholder confidence (High 92%) with all empty fields
notes: When extraction fails due to no-API-key early exit (db.jsx:162-165), the placeholder doc d is returned directly with default confidence 0.92 from makeDoc(). This is the 0.92 seeded in data.jsx:240. Fix: set d.confidence=0, d.fields=[] (or clear field values) when marking d.status='failed' in the no-key path.
verification: failed/no-key/no-blob paths set confidence:null + cleared fields; ConfidenceBadge renders nothing for null → no fake % on failed docs

title: [BUG-P0] Approve/Reject buttons active for Failed documents in workbench
verification: workbench.jsx Save draft / Reject / Approve buttons all carry disabled: doc.status === 'failed'
notes: workbench.jsx has no guard on doc.status==='failed' for the Approve/Reject buttons. Clicking Approve on a Failed doc triggers the validation-error toast but Reject actually works. Fix: disable both Approve and Reject buttons (or hide them) when doc.status==='failed'.

title: [BUG-P0] Agent steps never set when 'no API key' early-exit path in db.jsx
verification: commitBatch no-key AND no-blob branches now set d.agent_steps=[{type:'failed',summary:...}] + clear fields/confidence; reprocessDoc no-key/no-blob branches also set agent_steps
notes: db.jsx:162-165 early-exits with the placeholder doc d without calling runDocumentAgent. The doc has no agent_steps field. The workbench Agent tab shows 'No agent steps recorded' with no explanation of why it failed. Fix: set d.agent_steps=[{type:'failed',name:'',summary:'No API key for '+provider+' — add one in Settings.'}] in the no-key path.

title: [BUG-P1] OCR provider label shows historical extraction model, not current settings
notes: workbench.jsx:245-249 shows doc.ocr_provider (e.g. 'gemini (gemini-3.1-flash-lite)') in a badge that users read as the current model setting. In reality it is frozen at extraction time. Fix: change visible label text from 'OCR: ...' to 'Extracted with:' and add a clearer tooltip explaining it is historical.

title: [BUG-P1] Model 'gemini-3.1-flash-lite' in .env.sample is not in GEMINI_MODELS dropdown
notes: screens2.jsx GEMINI_MODELS list does not include gemini-3.1-flash-lite. If a user has this stored in IndexedDB (from old settings), the Select shows blank. Fix: either add gemini-3.1-flash-lite to GEMINI_MODELS list OR on Settings load, if stored model is not in the list, reset to default gemini-3.5-flash.
verification: screens2.jsx GEMINI_MODELS includes gemini-3.1-flash-lite (verified in Settings dropdown)

title: [BUG-P1] Notification bell button has no onClick handler
notes: ui.jsx:118 renders the bell button with no onClick. Clicking does nothing. Fix: add onClick that shows a 'No notifications' toast or dropdown panel. At minimum disable the button if notifications are not implemented.

title: [BUG-P1] Avatar user menu has no onClick handler; ChevronDown implies non-existent dropdown
notes: ui.jsx:119-122 renders 'Aishah Tan', 'Finance · Reviewer', ChevronDown icon — all hardcoded demo data with no onClick. Fix: either implement a basic profile dropdown or remove ChevronDown and make it non-interactive. Also the user name/role should be configurable in Settings.

title: [BUG-P1] 'Demo Mode' badge in TopBar is hardcoded and misleading
notes: ui.jsx:117 always shows 'Demo Mode' label even when real API keys are configured and real OCR has run. Fix: rename to 'Local Mode' (since data is stored locally, this is always true) or remove entirely. If kept, make it dynamic: show 'Live Mode' when a real key is saved.

title: [BUG-P1] Chinese debug text visible to end users in workbench
verification: workbench.jsx db-realnote now reads English: 'Box positions are layout estimates — they will update to exact AI-grounded positions after successful OCR.'
notes: workbench.jsx:280 renders Chinese text '⚠ 框为版面估算坐标 — Gemini 返回真实坐标后会自动定位到原文位置' to end users. This is a developer note left in production. Fix: replace with English UX copy e.g. '⚠ Box positions are estimated — they will update to exact AI-grounded positions after OCR.'

title: [BUG-P2] Default batch name has hardcoded 'June' instead of dynamic current month
notes: screens1.jsx:77 uses 'June Purchase Orders ' + new Date().toLocaleDateString(...) — 'June' is literal text hardcoded, so in July it would still say 'June Purchase Orders 14 Jul'. Fix: use new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-GB',{month:'long'}) + ' Purchase Orders ' + new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-GB',{day:'2-digit',month:'short'}).
verification: screens1.jsx default batch name uses current month via toLocaleDateString({month:'long'})

title: [BUG-P2] No OCR progress feedback while extraction is running
notes: After clicking Start OCR, docs immediately show 'processing' status but there is no spinner, progress bar, or time estimate. User cannot tell if OCR is running or stuck (especially for 30-90s Gemini calls). Fix: add a pulsing/spinning indicator on processing-status doc rows, and a note 'OCR in progress — may take up to 90 seconds per document.'
verification: screens2.jsx DocTable shows an 'OCR in progress — N documents extracting…' banner with a pulsing dot whenever any doc is processing (verified in preview)

title: [BUG-P2] Gemini OCR over-extracts line items (e.g. 28 items → 42 extracted)
notes: providers.jsx:286-288 prompt says 'containing EVERY row across ALL pages'. Gemini counts header rows, separator lines, summary/total rows, and blank rows as line items. Fix: tighten prompt to 'only rows that represent actual products or services — each must have at minimum a description. Skip blank rows, column header rows, subtotal rows, and section dividers.' [UPDATE 2026-06-14] v3 PDF is 3 pages; pdf.js text shows rows numbered to 28 on pp.1-2 and p.3 continues, so 42 extracted may be CORRECT, not over-extraction. Prompt now explicitly EXCLUDES blank/header/section/subtotal/grand-total rows. Needs a live recount with a real key to confirm the final count matches the visible product rows.

title: [BUG-P2] reprocessDoc in db.jsx calls LLMProviders directly, bypassing agent.jsx
notes: db.jsx:262 calls LLMProviders.ocrExtractWithFallback directly, so reprocessed docs never get agent_steps or agent_path set correctly. Fix: route reprocessDoc through window.DocAgent.runDocumentAgent like commitBatch does.
verification: db.jsx reprocessDoc routes through window.DocAgent.runDocumentAgent (not LLMProviders directly)

title: [BUG-P3] favicon.ico 404
notes: No favicon.ico in project root. Simple fix: add <link rel='icon' href='assets/logomark.svg'> to index.html <head>.

title: [BUG-P3] Sidebar storage progress bar shows 6% even with 0 docs
notes: main.jsx:55 has pct: Math.min(96, Math.round(store.documents.length/240*100)+6) — the +6 offset means even 0 docs shows 6%. The % is also based on doc count not actual MB. Fix: remove the +6 offset; or base pct on (bytes/maxBytes*100).
verification: main.jsx storage pct = round(bytes / (500MB) * 100), +6 offset removed

title: [BUG-P3] Batch breadcrumb 'Batches' link uses href='#' instead of navigating
verification: BatchDetail page wraps breadcrumb clicks: e.target.closest('.db-pageheader__crumbs a') → go('batches')
notes: screens2.jsx:180 breadcrumbs: [{label:'Batches',href:'#'}] — clicking 'Batches' in the breadcrumb doesn't actually navigate. Fix: attach onClick to call go('batches') instead of using href.
